bibliographic_data.title: Tool-integrated Reinforcement Learning for Repo Deep Search
bibliographic_data.abstract: Issue localization, the process of identifying code locations that need modification to resolve software issues, is a critical yet challenging task in software development. The semantic gap between natural language issue descriptions and faulty code requires complex multi-hop reasoning through code dependencies. Existing LLM-based agents attempt to address this by integrating repository retrieval tools. However, this transforms issue localization into a demanding task we call Repo Deep Search, which requires the LLM to effectively utilize various repository retrieval tools throughout a multi-step reasoning and navigation process. To tackle this challenge, we present ToolTrain, a two-stage tool-integrated training framework combining rejection-sampled supervised fine-tuning and tool-integrated reinforcement learning to enhance LLMs’ ability to use retrieval tools for issue localization. Experimental results show that ToolTrain-trained models achieve state-of-the-art performance, with our 32B model even surpassing Claude-3.7 on function-level localization. The results also show that improved localization performance translates to better end-to-end issue resolution performance.

core_content.novelty_claim: ToolTrain, a two-stage tool-integrated training framework combining rejection-sampled supervised fine-tuning and tool-integrated reinforcement learning, enhances LLMs’ ability to use retrieval tools for issue localization.
core_content.key_methodology: ToolTrain uses RepoSearcher, a lightweight and LLM-friendly issue localization agent, which includes simple retrieval tools. The model is trained in two stages: (1) Rejection-sampled supervised fine-tuning where the LLM generates tool-use trajectories, and only those leading to correct answers are used for fine-tuning; (2) Tool-integrated reinforcement learning where the LLM learns through trial and error by exploring different tool-use strategies. Rule-based reward signals evaluate whether the LLM’s exploration successfully locates the correct code elements.
outcomes: ToolTrain-trained models achieve state-of-the-art performance on issue localization tasks, surpassing Claude-3.7 on function-level localization.
Improved localization performance translates to better end-to-end issue resolution performance.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Issue localization is a critical task in software development that involves identifying code locations needing modification based on natural language descriptions of issues. Existing LLM-based agents integrate repository retrieval tools but struggle with the complex, multi-step reasoning required for Repo Deep Search. To address this, ToolTrain proposes a two-stage training framework combining rejection-sampled supervised fine-tuning and tool-integrated reinforcement learning. The model is trained using RepoSearcher, which includes simple retrieval tools and is trained in two stages: (1) Rejection-sampled supervised fine-tuning where the LLM generates tool-use trajectories, and only those leading to correct answers are used for fine-tuning; (2) Tool-integrated reinforcement learning where the LLM learns through trial and error by exploring different tool-use strategies. The results show that ToolTrain-trained models outperform existing methods on issue localization tasks.
